learning_outcomes.description: Assess historiographical evidence from primary sources regarding the nature and status of rights and freedoms, including the relationship between rights, freedoms, and other principles of law
Position primary sources in relation to the dominant intellectual traditions of rights and freedoms
Use primary source evidence to build arguments
Demonstrate scholarly analytical and reasoning skills regarding the relationship between primary sources and debates regarding rights and freedoms
Demonstrate ability to communicate clearly and persuasively about rights and freedoms in written and spoken form

assessments.task_description: Students will be assessed on their participation in class. Grades are awarded based on the ability to provide convincing interpretations of set primary sources by presenting evidence drawn from the sources. No marks are awarded for general reflections that don't relate to the texts, unless these are specifically asked for in class. Additionally, no marks are awarded merely for seminar attendance, but 5 marks will be deducted from the overall participation grade for each seminar missed without cause.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: Students are required to submit a 2,000 word research essay based on a selected essay question.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: Each week students will sit a brief in-class quiz consisting of 5 questions. Students will complete 12 over the course of the semester, with the best 8 results counting towards the final grade.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Length: approx. 1,500 words

Students will be required to analyse a contemporary statement regarding rights and freedoms in terms of the intellectual traditions studied in this course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
